metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! I've found some fantastic activities perfect for a 3-year-old, from exploring city farms and museums to enjoying interactive theatre shows and outdoor adventures. Let's make some memories!
metadata.transportDisclaimer: Always check travel routes and plan for disruptions.

activities.title: Last Unicorn Airways (4+)
activities.description: An immersive installation and theatre show.
activities.whyItWorks: The immersive installation element will be captivating for a 3-year-old, though parental guidance is needed.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes from Hackney to White Horse Road
transport.tip: Check performance times suit nap schedule

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with animals and a community feel.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals! Always a winner. Plus, it's free and local.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Playground! Always a good energy burner. Specifically designed for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Direct bus routes to Stratford International or a short walk from Hackney Wick Overground
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Interactive museum designed specifically for children with collections of toys and childhood objects.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for children, lots of hands-on activities. Mini Museum for under 2s.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Can get crowded, avoid peak hours

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.address: No.1 Warehouse, West India Quay, London E14 4AL
activities.description: Learn about the history of London's waterfront through interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of hands-on activities and child-friendly exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to West India Quay

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Local, free, and has animals.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or bus

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.address: Pier Street, Isle of Dogs, London E14 3HP
activities.description: One of the largest city farms, with free entry and lots of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, large space for running around, lots of animals.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Mudchute

activities.title: Half Moon Theatre
activities.description: Children's theatre with various shows for different age groups.
activities.whyItWorks: Age-appropriate shows, shorter performances suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Mile End, then walk

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Explore the cherry and magnolia walks during blossom season.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor space, beautiful scenery, great for walking and exploring. Sounds of Blossom.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and Overground to Kew Gardens

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Rd, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits about science, medicine, and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for young children. Pattern Pod for sensory exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to South Kensington
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Rd, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, animals, and the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: Dippy the Dinosaur and other exhibits are fascinating for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to South Kensington
transport.tip: Book in advance despite free entry

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Great views and greenery. Sensory experience.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Monument

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, animals, and a park for running around.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Golders Green, then a short walk
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated to children with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space for kids to let off steam, plus a paddling pool for warmer days.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Russell Square or King's Cross
transport.tip: Adults only allowed if accompanying a child

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world history and culture.
activities.whyItWorks: Family desk with activity sheets and Explorer Backpacks.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Tottenham Court Road or Holborn
transport.tip: Book in advance despite free entry

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A large playground with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship.
activities.whyItWorks: Pirate ship and sensory trail.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Notting Hill Gate or Queensway

activities.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
activities.description: A performance of A Midsummer Night's Dream suitable for children.
activities.whyItWorks: An introduction to Shakespeare in a child-friendly environment.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line
transport.tip: Check show times for best availability

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and imaginative.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Follow clues to find bunny statues and win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor, active, chocolate!

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: An interactive trail with challenges to protect the planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a popular children's show.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and Overground to Kew Gardens

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search for hidden eggs around the zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals and an Easter egg hunt!

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A stage production of Alice in Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic story brought to life on stage.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Hammersmith

activities.title: Fairy Tales at the British Library
activities.description: An immersive exhibition that invites children to step into the magical world of classic fairy tales through beautiful illustrations, pop-up books, original manuscripts and even puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive elements such as a magic mirror, a genie and magical potions.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Euston
transport.tip: A lovely cultural option for a spring day in London

activities.title: Gozzle Nature Trail
activities.address: Queen Elizabeth Walk, Barnes, London SW13 9WT
activities.description: An interactive outdoor trail charting Gozzle the gosling's journey
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor adventure, combines nature with a fun trail. Has stepping stones and animal poses.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and bus
